description:
During active balancing:
  · Selected cells shall be discharged through balancing resistor
  · Balancing shall continue until delta < 20 mV or timeout reached
  · Balancing shall be suspended when pack current > 5 A
supplementary_info:
Function: Cell Balancing
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation

analysis.direction: triggering: balancing shall continue until delta < 20 mV or timeout reached; clearing: balancing shall be suspended when pack current > 5 A
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior of cell balancing where delta cell voltage is less than 20 mV and timeout has not been reached.
Test the boundary condition for delta cell voltage threshold, i.e., when delta cell voltage is exactly 20 mV.
Test the boundary condition for timeout threshold, i.e., when timeout has been reached but pack current is below 5 A.
Test the fault condition where pack current exceeds 5 A during active balancing.

cases.title: Cell Balancing with Delta Cell Voltage < 20 mV and No Timeout
cases.objective: To verify that cell balancing stops when the delta cell voltage is less than 20 mV and timeout has not been reached.
cases.precondition: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults. Selected cells are overvoltage and pack current < 5 A.
cases.postcondition: BMS returns to normal operating state with cell balancing stopped due to delta cell voltage threshold being met.

steps.action: Set the delta cell voltage threshold to a value less than 20 mV (e.g., r_CellOV_Threshold).
steps.expected: The BMS shall initiate cell balancing for selected cells.

steps.action: Simulate a condition where the pack current exceeds the threshold (pack_current_threshold) to trigger suspension of balancing.
steps.expected: The BMS shall suspend cell balancing due to high pack current, but this step is not applicable as per requirement. No change in state expected here.

steps.action: Wait until the delta cell voltage falls below the threshold (r_CellOV_Threshold).
steps.expected: The BMS shall stop cell balancing when the delta cell voltage < r_CellOV_Threshold, and no timeout has been reached. [NEEDS REVIEW] The expected timing for this action is not specified.

cases.raw_html:
<testcase>
<title>Cell Balancing with Delta Cell Voltage < 20 mV and No Timeout</title>
<objective>To verify that cell balancing stops when the delta cell voltage is less than 20 mV and timeout has not been reached.</objective>
<precondition>BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults. Selected cells are overvoltage and pack current < 5 A.</precondition>
<steps>
<step order="1">
<action>Set the delta cell voltage threshold to a value less than 20 mV (e.g., r_CellOV_Threshold).</action>
<expected>The BMS shall initiate cell balancing for selected cells.</expected>
</step>
<step order="2">
<action>Simulate a condition where the pack current exceeds the threshold (pack_current_threshold) to trigger suspension of balancing.</action>
<expected>The BMS shall suspend cell balancing due to high pack current, but this step is not applicable as per requirement. No change in state expected here.</expected>
</step>
<step order="3">
<action>Wait until the delta cell voltage falls below the threshold (r_CellOV_Threshold).</action>
<expected>The BMS shall stop cell balancing when the delta cell voltage < r_CellOV_Threshold, and no timeout has been reached. [NEEDS REVIEW] The expected timing for this action is not specified.</expected>
</step>
</steps>
<postcondition>BMS returns to normal operating state with cell balancing stopped due to delta cell voltage threshold being met.</postcondition>
</testcase>

cases.title: Test the boundary condition for delta cell voltage threshold during active balancing
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS suspends balancing when the delta cell voltage reaches exactly 20 mV.
cases.precondition: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults. Selected cells are at a delta cell voltage of 19.99 mV and pack current is below 5 A.
cases.postcondition: BMS returns to normal operating state with no active faults, selected cells are not being discharged through the balancing resistor, and the system flag for active balancing is reset.

steps.action: Set the delta cell voltage to exactly 20 mV by applying an appropriate load or using a simulator.
steps.expected: The BMS shall initiate active balancing, discharging selected cells through the balancing resistor. The system flag for active balancing shall be set and the pack current monitored.

steps.action: Simulate a pack current of 5 A to trigger the suspension condition.
steps.expected: The BMS shall suspend active balancing due to the pack current exceeding the threshold, indicated by a change in the system flag for active balancing. The delta cell voltage threshold check shall be suspended until the pack current returns below the threshold.

steps.action: Wait for the debounce period (NEEDS REVIEW).
steps.expected: The BMS shall not resume active balancing during this period due to the debounce mechanism. After the debounce period, if the delta cell voltage remains above 20 mV and pack current is below the threshold, the system flag for active balancing shall be reset.

cases.raw_html:
<testcase>
<title>Test the boundary condition for delta cell voltage threshold during active balancing</title>
<objective>To verify that the BMS suspends balancing when the delta cell voltage reaches exactly 20 mV.</objective>
<precondition>BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults. Selected cells are at a delta cell voltage of 19.99 mV and pack current is below 5 A.</precondition>
<steps>
<step order="1">
<action>Set the delta cell voltage to exactly 20 mV by applying an appropriate load or using a simulator.</action>
<expected>The BMS shall initiate active balancing, discharging selected cells through the balancing resistor. The system flag for active balancing shall be set and the pack current monitored.</expected>
</step>
<step order="2">
<action>Simulate a pack current of 5 A to trigger the suspension condition.</action>
<expected>The BMS shall suspend active balancing due to the pack current exceeding the threshold, indicated by a change in the system flag for active balancing. The delta cell voltage threshold check shall be suspended until the pack current returns below the threshold.</expected>
</step>
<step order="3">
<action>Wait for the debounce period (NEEDS REVIEW).</action>
<expected>The BMS shall not resume active balancing during this period due to the debounce mechanism. After the debounce period, if the delta cell voltage remains above 20 mV and pack current is below the threshold, the system flag for active balancing shall be reset.</expected>
</step>
</steps>
<postcondition>BMS returns to normal operating state with no active faults, selected cells are not being discharged through the balancing resistor, and the system flag for active balancing is reset.</postcondition>
</testcase>

cases.title: Test the boundary condition for timeout threshold during cell balancing
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS suspends cell balancing when the timeout has been reached but the pack current is below 5 A.
cases.precondition: BMS initialized, all parameters within normal operating range, no active faults. Selected cells are at a delta voltage above 20 mV and pack current is below 5 A.
cases.postcondition: BMS returns to normal operating state with no active faults, and cell balancing is suspended if the timeout condition persists without a delta voltage drop below 20 mV.

steps.action: Set the delta cell voltage threshold to a value greater than 20 mV (e.g., r_CellOV_Threshold).
steps.expected: The BMS shall initiate active balancing on selected cells.

steps.action: Simulate a timeout condition by waiting for the timeout_threshold duration (e.g., t_Timeout_Debounce) to elapse without the delta cell voltage dropping below 20 mV.
steps.expected: [NEEDS REVIEW] The BMS shall suspend active balancing after the timeout_threshold duration has elapsed, but pack current remains below 5 A.

steps.action: Simulate a condition where the pack current exceeds 5 A (e.g., set pack_current_threshold to exceed 5 A).
steps.expected: The BMS shall resume active balancing on selected cells.

description:
Upon charger connection detection (CP/PP or CC signal):
  · Verify all safety preconditions
  · IF all preconditions satisfied
    THEN BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 1
  · ELSE BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0, report reason on CAN
supplementary_info:
Function: Charge Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation
Context: Charge initiation is gated by a set of safety preconditions verified after charger connection. The BMS signals readiness on CAN once all preconditions are satisfied. If any precondition is not met, the reason is reported on CAN for diagnostic and HMI purposes:
- BMS_ChargeReady_Flag = 1: charger detected, no active faults, temperature in range, pack voltage in range
- Preconditions checked: active faults (sections 1-8), temperature limits (section 3), OVP state (section 1), undervoltage (section 8)

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 1 if all preconditions are satisfied; BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0 if any precondition is not met.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Verify that BMS_ChargeReady_Flag is set to 1 when all safety preconditions are satisfied upon charger connection.
Verify that BMS_ChargeReady_Flag remains 0 if any single safety precondition is not met during charger connection detection.
Simulate an active fault condition and verify that BMS_ChargeReady_Flag is set to 0 with the specific fault reason reported on CAN.
Verify the transition of BMS_ChargeReady_Flag from 0 to 1 as safety preconditions are met in sequence during charger connection detection.
Ensure that the specific reason for BMS_ChargeReady_Flag being set to 0 is correctly reported on CAN when a single safety precondition fails.

steps.action: Simulate all safety preconditions being satisfied (no active faults, temperature within range, pack voltage within range)
steps.expected: BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 1; no CAN report [NEEDS REVIEW]

description:
While BMS_CellOT_Flag = 1 AND charging:
  · IF cell temperature > 45 C
    THEN BMS_ChargeDerating_Active := 1
  · Charge current shall be reduced proportionally to temperature rise
supplementary_info:
Function: Thermal Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_ChargeDerating_Active is set when the temperature condition is met.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test that BMS_ChargeDerating_Active is set to 1 when BMS_CellOT_Flag = 1, charging, and cell temperature > 45 C.
Test the boundary condition where cell temperature is exactly 45 C during charging while BMS_CellOT_Flag = 1.
Test that BMS_ChargeDerating_Active remains 0 when BMS_CellOT_Flag = 0, regardless of temperature or charging state.
Verify the transition from BMS_ChargeDerating_Active = 0 to BMS_ChargeDerating_Active = 1 as cell temperature crosses the threshold during charging with BMS_CellOT_Flag = 1.

description:
IF  any cell voltage <= 2.80 V
THEN
  · BMS_CellUV_Flag := 1
  · Limit discharge power
supplementary_info:
Function: Undervoltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response
Context: Undervoltage protection prevents cell damage from over-discharge by limiting power draw and, if the voltage continues to fall, opening the discharge contactor. A proportional power limit is applied between the warning and critical thresholds to give the load time to reduce demand before hard shutdown. Recovery requires cells to return above 3.00 V:
- BMS_CellUV_Flag = 1: any cell <= 2.80 V (proportional power limiting)
- BMS_CellUV_L2_Flag = 1: any cell <= 2.50 V (contactor open, discharge prohibited)

analysis.direction: triggering
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

Verify that BMS_CellUV_Flag remains active as long as at least one cell voltage stays <= 2.80 V.

description:
The BMS shall continuously track:
  · Capacity fade (current vs. rated capacity)
  · DC internal resistance trend
  · Cell voltage deviation under load
supplementary_info:
Function: Degradation Monitoring
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring > 11 Degradation Monitoring > 11.1 Cell Degradation Detection
Context: Degradation monitoring tracks long-term cell aging through multiple indicators to detect capacity loss and impedance increase before they affect system performance. The SOH prediction model is updated when any indicator exceeds its threshold:
- Capacity fade: current capacity vs. rated capacity
- Impedance growth: DC internal resistance trend
- Voltage deviation: cell voltage dispersion under load
- BMS_CellDegrade_Warn = 1: any indicator exceeds defined threshold

analysis.direction: The BMS should trigger the warning signal (BMS_CellDegrade_Warn) when any of the indicators exceed their respective thresholds.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where a single indicator exceeds its threshold, triggering BMS_CellDegrade_Warn.
Test the boundary condition where an indicator is exactly at its threshold value, ensuring it triggers BMS_CellDegrade_Warn.
Test a scenario where multiple indicators exceed their thresholds simultaneously to ensure proper warning and response behavior.
Verify the state transition from normal operation to degradation monitoring mode when any indicator exceeds its threshold.
Ensure that the BMS can accurately detect and report cell degradation through the BMS_CellDegrade_Warn signal under various load conditions.

description:
Upon VCU_HVDisconnect_Req or critical fault:
  · Open both main contactors
  · Discharge DC link capacitance
  · BMS_SystemState := HV_Deactivating
  · THEN Standby or SafeState as appropriate
supplementary_info:
Function: BMS State Machine
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring > 11 Degradation Monitoring > 11.1 Cell Degradation Detection > 12 Fault Management > 12.1 Unrecoverable Fault Handling > 13 BMS State Machine > 13.1 Power-Up and Initialization > 13.2 HV Activation > 13.3 HV Deactivation and Sleep
Context: HV deactivation is triggered by normal shutdown requests or fault conditions from any protection function (sections 1-12). After contactors open and the DC link discharges, the system may enter low-power sleep mode to conserve quiescent current while maintaining wake-up capability:
- Deactivation: open contactors, discharge DC link, transition through HV_Deactivating to Standby or SafeState
- Sleep entry: Standby with no CAN activity for configurable timeout
- Sleep exit: CAN activity or wake pin assertion

description:
IF  calculated SOH <= 80 %
THEN
  · BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn := 1
  · Store DTC P0A0F in non-volatile memory
supplementary_info:
Function: SOC/SOH Estimation
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring
Context: SOH degradation tracking enables early detection of battery aging and supports warranty analysis. Degradation is evaluated through multiple indicators including capacity fade and cumulative throughput. When the design cycle limit is exceeded, an SOH re-calibration is requested:
- BMS_BatteryDegrade_Warn = 1: SOH <= 80 %
- BMS_SOHEstFault_Flag = 1: cumulative throughput exceeds limit, DTC P0A0F stored

description:
IF  pack discharge current >= 300 A for >= 10 ms
THEN
  · BMS_SCD_Flag := 1
  · Open both main contactors immediately
  · BMS_SystemState := SafeState
supplementary_info:
Function: Current Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection
Context: Short circuit detection uses a higher threshold and shorter integration window than overcurrent detection, targeting hard faults such as harness shorts or inverter bridge shoot-through. The SCD response overrides all other current management actions and requires a diagnostic clear to recover:
- BMS_SCD_Flag = 1: discharge current >= 300 A for >= 10 ms
- BMS_SCD_Latch = 1: latched state persistent across power cycles

analysis.direction: triggering
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where the discharge current is exactly 300 A for exactly 10 ms, resulting in BMS_SCD_Flag being set to 1 and both main contactors opening immediately.
Test the boundary condition where the discharge current is slightly above 300 A (e.g., 300.1 A) for exactly 10 ms, ensuring BMS_SCD_Flag is set to 1 and both main contactors open.
Test the boundary condition where the discharge current is exactly 300 A but lasts less than 10 ms (e.g., 9.9 ms), ensuring BMS_SCD_Flag remains at 0 and no action is taken.
Test a fault scenario where the discharge current exceeds 300 A for more than 10 ms, verifying that BMS_SCD_Flag is set to 1, both main contactors open, and BMS_SystemState transitions to SafeState.

Test the state transition after a power cycle where BMS_SCD_Latch = 1, ensuring that BMS_SCD_Flag remains at 1 and all other current management actions are overridden until a diagnostic clear is performed.

cases.postcondition: System returned to normal operating state.

description:
IF  BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1 AND any cell voltage >= r_CellOV_L3_Threshold
THEN
  · BMS_CellOV_L3_Flag := 1
supplementary_info:
Function: Overvoltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery
Context: If the overvoltage condition escalates beyond Level 2 despite derating, immediate contactor opening is required to protect the battery. Contactors are opened in sequence and the system transitions to a safe state:
- BMS_ChargeContactor_State: 0 = Open, 1 = Closed
- BMS_DischargeContactor_State: 0 = Open, 1 = Closed
- BMS_SystemState transitions to SafeState; refer to section 13 for state definitions
- Recovery requires all cell voltages below 4.00 V for >= 5 s

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CellOV_L3_Flag := 1 when any cell voltage >= r_CellOV_L3_Threshold and BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where BMS_ChargeTerm_Req is active (1) and any cell voltage exceeds r_CellOV_L3_Threshold, resulting in BMS_CellOV_L3_Flag being set to 1.
Test the boundary condition where a single cell voltage just meets or slightly exceeds r_CellOV_L3_Threshold while BMS_ChargeTerm_Req is active (1).
Test the fault condition where multiple cells exceed r_CellOV_L3_Threshold and BMS_ChargeTerm_Req is active (1), ensuring immediate contactor opening.
Verify that after setting BMS_CellOV_L3_Flag, the system transitions to SafeState with all contactors opened.
Ensure observability of BMS_SystemState transitioning to SafeState and contactors being opened when any cell voltage exceeds r_CellOV_L3_Threshold while BMS_ChargeTerm_Req is active (1).

description:
IF  any cell temperature <= low temperature limit
THEN
  · BMS_CellUT_Flag := 1
  · BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0 (charging prohibited)
supplementary_info:
Function: Thermal Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection
Context: Low temperature protection prevents charging when cells are too cold to accept current without damage, and manages the battery heater to enable charging under cold conditions. The heater is de-energized on overtemperature or when no longer needed:
- BMS_CellUT_Flag = 1: any cell <= low temperature limit (charging prohibited, BMS_ChargeReady_Flag = 0)
- BMS_HeaterOT_Flag = 1: heater temperature exceeds safe limit
- Heater activation threshold: cell <= -10 C during charge session

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CellUT_Flag := 1 when any cell temperature <= low temperature limit; clearing: BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0 (charging prohibited)
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where a single cell temperature is below the low temperature limit, causing BMS_CellUT_Flag to be set and BMS_ChargeReady_Flag to be cleared.
Test the boundary condition where exactly one cell temperature equals the low temperature limit threshold (if such information were provided).
Test the scenario where multiple cells are below the low temperature limit, ensuring BMS_CellUT_Flag is set and BMS_ChargeReady_Flag remains cleared.
Test the state transition from normal operation to under-temperature protection when a cell temperature drops below the threshold.
Verify that the system correctly updates BMS_CellUT_Flag and BMS_ChargeReady_Flag in response to changes in cell temperatures.
